notes: Git commit objects hosted on GitHub. Commits are identified by SHA-1 hashes (40 hex characters), though abbreviated forms (7+ characters) are widely used. Because commit hashes are content-addressed, the same hash uniquely identifies the same commit across forks and mirrors. Resolution uses GitHub's commit search, which finds the commit in any public repository.

match_nodes.description: Full SHA-1 commit hash (40 hex characters)

data.notes: Abbreviated hashes may match multiple commits across repositories. GitHub search returns all matches. Prefer full 40-character hashes for unambiguous resolution.

examples.note: Short hash — may match multiple commits
